title: hieracium onosmoides subsp. suboreades
intro: Hieracium onosmoides subsp. suboreades, also known as Hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family, native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Hawkweed include its ability to reduce swelling and pain, as well as its potential to treat wounds, fever, and skin conditions. Hawkweed can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
